title: Combinatorics and Geometry of the Amplituhedron
speaker: Lauren Williams

proof_stages.label: Positive Grassmannian and positroid cells
proof_stages.summary: The talk sets up Grassmannians via Plücker coordinates and matroids, then explains that imposing nonnegativity turns the badly behaved matroid stratification into the positroid cell decomposition by open balls.

proof_stages.label: Amplituhedron as positive-Grassmannian image motivated by amplitudes
proof_stages.summary: After motivating positivity from applications and scattering amplitudes, the amplituhedron is defined as the image of Gr_{[33m≥[0m0}(k,n) under multiplication by a totally positive matrix Z, with BCFW recurrences interpreted geometrically as tilings whose internal boundaries explain spurious poles.

proof_stages.label: Benchmark specializations of the amplituhedron
proof_stages.summary: Special cases identify the amplituhedron with the positive Grassmannian, polygons and cyclic polytopes, or bounded complexes of cyclic hyperplane arrangements, providing geometric anchors and later Q&A context about extremality.

proof_stages.label: Tiles, tilings, and twistor coordinates
proof_stages.summary: To study volume one restricts to km-dimensional positroid cells on which the amplituhedron map is injective, calls their images tiles, defines tilings by disjoint covering, and introduces Z-dependent twistor coordinates for facet inequalities.

proof_stages.label: Cluster-variable facet principle
proof_stages.summary: Small m=1 and m=2 tile calculations lead into cluster-algebra background and the central conjecture that tile facets are cut out by compatible cluster variables for Gr(m,n), with each tile described by fixed signs of those variables.

proof_stages.label: Verified tile and tiling results for m=2 and BCFW m=4
proof_stages.summary: The cluster-facet statement is proved for all m=2 tiles and for all BCFW m=4 tiles, while BCFW collections are shown to tile the amplituhedron, with cluster coordinates playing a key role in injectivity and disjointness.

proof_stages.label: Magic-number tiling count and m=2 proof strategy
proof_stages.summary: The enumerative evidence is organized into the McMahon magic-number conjecture for the common size of even-m tilings, proved for m=2 by classifying tiles and constructing a constant additive Parke–Taylor invariant, with higher-m behavior left largely open.

examples.name: Polygon and cyclic-polytope special cases
examples.note: For k=1,m=2 the amplituhedron is a polygon, and for k=1 general m it is a cyclic polytope; this makes the definition tangible and later sanity-checks the magic-number count by recovering n-2 triangles for an n-gon.
examples.engagement_reason: she explains concretely why the k=1,m=2 amplituhedron is a polygon via nonnegative linear combinations of the rows of Z, and notes the cyclic-polytope generalization
